author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.

classification.cautionReason: This work discusses heresy in order to refute it; do not classify it as heretical merely from its title.

lineage: Writings in Connection with the Donatist Controversy.
Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
In which Augustin replies to all the several statements in the letter of Petilianus, as though disputing with an adversary face to face.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Petilianus said: "But what reason is there, or what inconsistency of emptiness, in desiring communion with us so eagerly, when all the time you call us by the false title of heretics?"

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Augustin answered: If we so eagerly desired communion with heretics, we should not be anxious that you should be converted from the error of heresy; but when the very object of our negotiations with you is that you should cease to be heretics, how are we eagerly desiring communion with heretics?

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: For, in fact, it is dissension and division that make you heretics; but peace and unity make men Catholics.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: When, then, you come over from your heresy to us, you cease to be what we hate, and begin to be what we love.
